mainEntity.name: Does the law force me to see the 'company doctor' after I tripped and hurt my knee?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah and Idaho, employers generally have the right to choose the treating physician. You should consult a lawyer before switching doctors on your own to avoid losing coverage for medical bills.
acceptedAnswer.description: In Utah, for the first visit, you can often choose your doctor, but the employer or their insurance carrier generally has the right to designate the treating physician thereafter. This is a critical point of friction because "company doctors" are often perceived as being incentivized to return you to work quickly. In Idaho, the employer/surety has the right to select the physician in most cases. If you are unhappy with the care, you cannot simply switch on your own without risking non-payment of bills. You must follow specific legal procedures to request a change of physician. A lawyer can help you navigate this process to ensure you see a specialist who actually prioritizes your recovery.
